tools.name: publish
tools.description: Publish Markdown or HTML as a hosted dochost page and get a shareable URL. Respects your plan: link lifetime, password protection, custom slug, and branding all follow your account entitlements.

tools.name: list_my_pages
tools.description: List the pages you have published to dochost, newest first. Paginated; returns compact records (no page bodies).

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Get your plan, page-quota usage, and entitlement flags (size cap, custom slug, password, branding). Call before publishing so you know your limits up front.

tools.name: get_page
tools.description: Get one of your pages by slug: title, format, status, view/like counts, expiry, and whether it is password-protected. Never returns the page body or the password.

tools.name: update_page
tools.description: Replace the content of one of your pages in place. The URL, view/like counts, and expiry stay the same; only the body, format, and title change. Resending identical content is a no-op.

tools.name: delete_page
tools.description: Permanently delete one of your pages by slug. The link stops working immediately and the slug is freed. Deleting an already-deleted page is a safe no-op.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "publish" references sensitive artifacts (password). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Publish Markdown or HTML as a hosted dochost page and get a shareable URL. Respects your plan: link lifetime, password protection, custom slug, and branding all…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: publish, update_page · Private data: publish, list_my_pages, get_account, get_page · Public sink: publish · Destructive: get_page, update_page, delete_page.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: publish, update_page · Private data: publish, list_my_pages, get_account, get_page · Public sink: publish · Destructive: get_page, update_page, delete_page

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
